data.title: Honda Stock (HMC) Feels Heat as $15.7B EV Reckoning Roils U.S., China Plans
data.description:
Honda Motor Company's (HMC) shares continued to falter early Friday as the Japanese automaker brace for a 2.5 trillion yen ($15.7 billion) charge in restructuring costs for its electric vehicle business. Honda is also expecting its first annual loss in nearly 70 years, even as it writes down the value of its Chinese assets and looks to shelve certain EV models planned for production in the U.S. and across North America.

data.title: Why Did Investors Lose Faith In Immutep Stock (IMMP) Today?
data.description:
Immutep (IMMP) stock fell hard on Friday after the biotechnology company announced it would discontinue a Phase III clinical trial. This study was focused on eftilagimod alfa, a treatment in development to treat patients with first-line non-small cell lung cancer.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Immutep said that it will stop enrollment in the study and wind it down on the recommendation of the Independent Data Monitoring Committee (IDMC) for the trial. This recommendation was made following an interim futility analysis of the trial. The IDMC reached a discontinue-due-to-futility conclusion after going over the safety and efficacy data for the trial.

Marc Voigt, CEO of Immutep, said, "We are very disappointed and surprised with the outcome of the futility analysis, in light of efti's performance in every other clinical trial. We would like to thank the patients, investigators, and clinical teams who contributed to this important study. We are currently conducting a comprehensive review of the available data to better understand the results and determine the appropriate next steps for the program."

What's Next for Immutep?

Immutep said it will continue to focus on the other candidates in its pipeline following the end of this Phase III clinical trial. It also noted that with the end of this trial, the company's cash runway has been extended to Q2 2027. Immutep intends to provide an updated outlook on its revised cash runway and will reassess capital allocation priorities once it's done closing down the eftilagimod alfa study.

Investors Lose Faith in Immutep Stock

Immutep's decision to end its clinical trial was a major blow to investor morale. This saw the stock plummet 79.26% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 1.1% rally yesterday. The shares were down 3.5% year-to-date but were up 55.06% over the past 12 months.

Today's news also appears to have triggered a selloff of IMMP stock, as more than 11 million shares have changed hands this morning. That's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 154,000 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Marvell's Optics Ambitions Set for 'Pockets of Upside' — Where Do Analysts See MRVL Stock?
data.description:
Marvell's (MRVL) shares edged higher early Friday after two five-star analysts backed the U.S. semiconductor company's race to develop optical or light-based links that move data between chips and systems. The analysts reaffirmed their Buy rating on MRVL and lifted their price targets, citing significant upside.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As a semiconductor company, Marvell designs and makes chips for data storage, networking, and connectivity. In terms of connectivity, it offers Ethernet solutions such as controllers and network adapters.

Marvell Set for 'High-Stakes' Micro-LED Optics Battle

Stifel analyst Tore Svanberg remains bullish on the company's optical connectivity goals and raised his price target from $114 to $120, implying about 37% upside.

Svanberg contended that Marvell's long-term partnership with tech startup Mojo Vision — which was announced on Thursday — positions the company for the "high-stakes" race to build and deploy micro-LED-based optical interconnects at large scale at data centers. Both companies plan to work together to build this technology over several hardware generations.

The five-star analyst also believes that the new partnership could help boost the benefits of Marvell's acquisition of Celestial AI, a photonic technology startup. The startup uses light instead of copper wires to accelerate data transfer between servers. The acquisition was announced in December and completed last month.

Marvell's plan and timeline for its silicon photonics products and technologies also stand to benefit as well, the analyst noted.

UBS Flags Gains from Optical Interconnect Transition

On his part, UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri pointed to "pockets of upsides" for Marvell, including its effort to develop chip-to-chip data movement using light over fiber. Arcuri sees gains from a top cloud customer's plan to begin deploying this technology starting in 2027.

The five-star analyst similarly put a $120 price target on Marvell's shares. Other areas flagged for gains include:

  • High pricing for Marvell's custom ASIC chip — up to several thousand dollars per chip — and orders from Microsoft (MSFT)
  • Revenue of around 44% from Marvell's interconnect business segment, even as the company plans an increase of between 15% and 20% in capital expenditure over the next year

Is Marvell a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Across Wall Street, analysts remain highly bullish on Marvell's shares and have a Strong Buy rating on the stock. This is based on 22 Buys and five Holds issued over the past three months.

In addition, the average MRVL price target of $122.52 implies about 40% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Why Is iSpecimen Stock (ISPC) Up Today 3/13/26?
data.description:
iSpecimen (ISPC) stock saw a major rally on Friday after the technology marketplace that connects researchers with biospecimen suppliers announced the launch of an AI agent. This AI agent is focused on the company's inventory and is designed to help speed up the review process of incoming biospecimen requests and match them with available inventory from suppliers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

iSpecimen noted that this AI agent is the first in a planned expansion of features on its platform. The company also intends to leverage AI to introduce automated monitoring and validation of regulatory submissions, further automation of high-compliance and data-intensive workflows, and intelligent lead qualification and outreach management. All of this is designed to accelerate access to critical research samples while making the process easier.

Katie Field, CEO of iSpecimen, said, "Optimizing the marketplace for human biospecimens has been the founding mission of iSpecimen since day one. The launch of our AI agent marks a significant milestone in that journey, one that we believe will meaningfully enhance outcomes for our suppliers, our customers, and ultimately the researchers advancing scientific discovery around the world."

iSpecimen Joins the AI Agent Trend

iSpecimen is far from the only company that has jumped on the AI agent bandwagon. Several companies have developed these agents to take over and automate tasks. In particular, there's been a trend of adopting these agents in the healthcare space.

Case in point are new AI agents launched by Amazon (AMZN) and Microsoft (MSFT) earlier this week. Amazon launched a healthcare consultant agent for its Prime subscribers to assist them with their medical needs. Not to be left out, Microsoft made a similar announcement just a couple of days later when it introduced Copilot Health.

iSpecimen Stock Rockets Today

iSpecimen stock was up 77.99% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 6.67% fall yesterday. The shares have also decreased 13.85% year-to-date and 83.28% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of ISPC stock, as more than 36 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month average daily trading volume is about 8.88 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Oracle Stock (ORCL) Climbs After Q3 Shows AI Strategy Taking Hold — I'm Bullish
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) delivered a strong Fiscal Q3, suggesting its artificial intelligence (AI) strategy is starting to pay off. The quarter highlighted rising demand for Oracle's cloud and AI infrastructure services, reinforcing the company's shift from a traditional SaaS vendor to a major AI infrastructure provider. With massive AI contract backlogs and growing cloud momentum, I remain bullish on ORCL's longer-term prospects.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company's revenue and bookings reflected strong demand from hyperscale clients and partners, including OpenAI. This comes after last August's "nuclear" remaining performance obligations (RPO)—a backlog of nearly half a trillion dollars in AI contracts—that had pushed shares to record highs of $328 per share. While questions remain about execution and the capital intensity required to convert this backlog into revenue, the Q3 performance reassures investors that Oracle is executing on its AI strategy.

For a "SaaS-oriented" company that does not have cash flows as robust as those of big tech hyperscalers such as Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOGL), concerns around AI spending hit Oracle harder than its peers. With a leaner balance sheet and a higher concentration of AI contracts in a single customer, ORCL shares fell more than 50% from their peak.

After a rough start to 2026, with shares down over 23% through early March, Q3 earnings have eased bearish momentum, prompting a positive market reaction to recent developments. Delving deeper into what Oracle delivered in Q3 and assessing its valuation amid this transition toward becoming an AI cloud provider, I present several arguments in this article that support a bullish position on ORCL.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Inch Lower as Investors Assess Oil Prices, Await PCE Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended lower early Friday as investors assessed the movement in oil prices amid the escalating Iran war. Also, focus will be on January's personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index reading, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, scheduled for release this morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.15%, 0.07%, and 0.04%, respectively, at 5:22 a.m. EST on March 13.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Despite assurances from the Trump administration and the International Energy Agency (IEA) about releasing oil reserves, concerns about oil prices remain as Iran continues to disrupt supply. In fact, Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, said that the crucial Strait of Hormuz will remain shut as a "tool to pressure the enemy." At the time of writing, the price of the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up about 0.45% to trade at $100.91 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) was essentially flat at $95.80 per barrel. Iran has warned that oil prices could climb to $200 a barrel.  

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major indexes plunged amid a surge in oil prices and escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The Dow Jones fell about 740 points to end the day below the 47,000 mark for the first time this year. The S&P 500 fell 1.5%, while the Nasdaq Composite declined 1.78%.

Coming to Friday's key economic release, the headline PCE is expected to have gained 0.3% month-over-month and 2.9% year-over-year.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was essentially flat at 4.273%, as of the last check. Also, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price was around $5,088 per ounce.

Elsewhere, European indexes were in the red as investors digested updates on the ongoing U.S.-Iran war and its broader impact.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Friday

Asia-Pacific markets closed lower on March 13 amid fears that higher oil prices could trigger a global economic slowdown.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index declined 0.98%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.82%, while the Shenzhen Component closed 0.65% lower. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei declined 1.16%, with the Topix falling 0.57%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Target Needs More Than Price Cuts to Create a 'Winning Playbook' For Customers, Warn Analysts
data.description:
Retailer Target's (TGT) sweeping round of price cuts this week is unlikely to see customers flocking back to its stores.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Price Cuts Not Enough

CFRA analyst Arun Sundaram said the price cuts on over 3,000 products were a "step in the right direction, but they alone are not enough to win back customers. The winning playbook is broader than simply lowering prices."

The company is reducing prices by between 5% and 20% on many of its products starting this month and lasting through spring. They include clothes, home bedding and blankets, shoes and everyday essentials such as baby items and pantry staples.

Target's price-cut plans come after the company has suffered significant decreases in traffic. It saw foot traffic decrease by 2% year-over-year in Q4 2025. Instead of shopping at Target, consumers have turned to its rivals, such as Walmart (WMT), instead.

Aside from tough competition, Target has also been hit by issues with inventory and complaints concerning store conditions.

Will Investors Be Patient?

Its revenues have fallen for five straight quarters, while operating income has dropped for the past three even though the year-over-year pace of decline has slowed.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: 'Get Your Shopping Lists Ready': Morgan Stanley Suggests 2 Stocks to Buy as Market Weakness Could Create Opportunity
data.description:
The war with Iran has become a clear backdrop to the market's recent weakness. Rising tensions in the region bring a familiar set of worries – geopolitical instability, possible disruptions to global oil supplies, and the broader uncertainty that tends to follow war. None of that tends to do equities any favors.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

But that's not the whole story. Mike Wilson, chief US equity strategist from Morgan Stanley, noted that liquidity was tightening and that markets were shifting toward correction territory. In his opinion, the current war has simply brought the stressors into sharper focus. That said, Wilson believes that investors can find opportunities in the near term as the market bottoms out this spring.

"The questions for equity investors now are what will the world look like in six months and are prices cheap enough to start assuming a better future? The short answer is not yet, but get your shopping lists ready… I think the S&P 500 could trade toward 6300 by early April before our favorable fundamental outlook can take hold again," Wilson opined.

Wilson's is the long-term upbeat view, and his colleagues among the Morgan Stanley stock analysts are busy picking out stocks to buy in a weaker market environment. We've opened up the TipRanks platform to look up the details on two of those picks – here they are, along with the Morgan Stanley commentaries.

Flywire Corporation (FLYW)

We'll start in the world of online payment processing, where Flywire has, for over 15 years, offered a wide range of online digital payment solutions. The company operates around the world, providing services for the education, travel, and hospital/health sectors. Flywire's platform lets international companies operate smoothly in a multi-currency global environment, taking payments in their customers' currencies and using their own local currencies to settle their own accounts.

The company works with more than 4,900 global clients, and its service covers more than 240 countries and territories and over 140 currencies. At its core, the Flywire service is a desirable combination of vital advantages: secure transactions, low payment costs, and simplified reconciliations.

In an important development, Flywire last year acquired Sertifi, the cloud-based online electronic signature platform. The acquisition cost Flywire $330 million, and Sertifi provided $14.2 million in revenue for Flywire during 4Q25.

The popularity of Flywire's service can be seen in a key metric from the company's 4Q25 report. In that release, Flywire reported that its total payment volume increased by 35.6% year-over-year, from $6.9 billion in 4Q24 to $9.3 billion in the final quarter of 2025.

Elsewhere in the 4Q25 report, Flywire reported generating $157.5 million at the top line in the quarter, for a 34% year-over-year increase and beating the estimates by $7.74 million. At the bottom line, Flywire's GAAP EPS came to $0.00 – and that figure beat the forecast by a penny. During Q4, Flywire paid down the remaining debt, $15 million, which it had incurred in the Sertifi acquisition.

Morgan Stanley analyst Michael Infante sees Flywire in a sound position, based on recent performance. He writes of the company, "We think Flywire has earned back credibility in the public markets, with four straight quarters of MSD+ RLAS beats, and with the '26 outlook still assuming meaningful visa headwinds, we think the stage is set for continued upward estimate revisions, which should warrant multiple expansion… In our view, the best evidence of Flywire's share gain and cross-sell success is the spread between visa growth and revenue growth in key Education markets, as Flywire's results in '25 demonstrate the company's ability to deliver robust results in a fairly punitive visa regime."

Quantifying his stance, Infante rates the shares as Overweight (i.e., Buy), and sets a price target of $17 that suggests a one-year upside potential of 36%. (To watch Infante's track record, click here)

The 12 recent analyst reviews here break down to 7 Buys, 4 Holds, and 1 Sell, for a Moderate Buy consensus rating. The stock is priced at $12.49 and its $16.60 average target price indicates a 33% upside in store for the coming year. (See FLYW stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus
